subtitle: Offline-first weighing app (Expo/Electron client + Node-RED hub) — one system.
remediatedNote: This review's fixes (auto-update, backdoor, SQLi) lift it to ~53; clearing the backlog targets a B.

dimensions.name: Security & secrets
dimensions.note: Hub backdoor + unauthenticated admin API + client auto-update RCE; cleartext transport. Weakest-link dominates.

dimensions.name: Data integrity & sync
dimensions.note: Convergence fails; 2 Critical silent-write-loss paths; wall-clock LWW; non-idempotent hub apply. Weakest-link: scored as the failing half, not averaged.

dimensions.name: Architecture & code quality
dimensions.note: Client clean (repo layer, typed); hub poor (4.3/10 — god function-nodes, deployed!=tested, 15x dup).

dimensions.name: Data layer
dimensions.note: Client B+ (FK on, transactional migrations); hub C (untyped EAV blob, no FK/migrations).

dimensions.name: API & contracts
dimensions.note: No versioning/OpenAPI; race-prone invoice allocation; RPC verbs; inconsistent envelopes.

dimensions.name: Testing & verifiability
dimensions.note: Tests authentic where present, but ~19% line coverage; hub's tested lib isn't the deployed code.

dimensions.name: UX & accessibility
dimensions.note: Strong IA + design system; WCAG gaps (contrast, label association, dialog focus).

dimensions.name: UI / code consistency
dimensions.note: Client token system solid; hub flow has heavy duplication / drift.

critical.title: Auto-updater RCE — unsigned installer, no integrity check, HTTP allowed
Silent sync data loss (un-retried skip C1 + markSynced race C2)
critical.impact: A committed write can vanish / never replicate

next: Fix the two sync data-loss paths — dead-letter for skipped rows + guarded markSynced.
Enable adminAuth on the hub; rotate any NutSync2026! account.
TLS on the hub + reject Bearer over plaintext; bump vulnerable dependencies.
Add a deterministic sync fault-injection test harness.
